content:
One SEO issue that is easy to miss in modern stacks: the HTML Googlebot receives is not always the same page users think they shipped.
Before launching a page that matters for organic traffic, I like to check three versions:

• View-source HTML

• Rendered DOM in the browser

• What Search Console’s URL inspection or a crawler sees after JS rendering
Things I specifically look for:

• Title and meta description present before/after render

• One clear canonical URL

• Primary H1 in the rendered page

• Internal links available as crawlable anchors, not only click handlers

• Product/service copy present in HTML, not hidden behind delayed client-side fetches

• No accidental noindex from staging/config
It sounds basic, but it catches a lot of “we built a nice page and Google ignored it” problems. The fix is usually not magic SEO work. It is making the important content and links unambiguous to crawlers.

  submitted by   /u/VRTCLS
